feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Orchestration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Orchestration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP PO WSSE Security in SOAP adapter
entry.content:
Hi
I am using the SOAP receiver adapter in SAP PO. The webservice i consume needs the Username and Password in the Soap Header like this:

	<soap:Header>
        <wsse:Security soap:mustUnderstand="1" xmlns:wsse="http://docs.oasis-open.org/wss/2004/01/oasis-200401-wss-wssecurity-secext-1.0.xsd">
            <wsse:UsernameToken>
                <wsse:Username>xxx</wsse:Username>
                <wsse:Password>yyy</wsse:Password>
            </wsse:UsernameToken>
        </wsse:Security>
      </soap:Header>

How do i do that?

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration - Best approach to connect SAP PO 7.5 to CPI HTTPS endpoint with CSRF enabled
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to connect from SAP PO 7.5 (using the REST adapter) to a CPI HTTPS endpoint where CSRF protection is enabled.
Our goal is to achieve this integration with minimal changes to the existing SAP PO iFlow.

  • What is the recommended approach to handle the CSRF token in this scenario?

  • Is it necessary to implement an Async-Sync Bridge on the PO side, or are there more efficient alternatives to manage the token exchange?

Any guidance, best practices, or references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance,

Niranjan Nayak

entry.title: Unable to deploy Function Library while Migrating from SAP PO to SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While working on a B2B migration from SAP PO to SAP CPI, I am adding the Mapping and relevant Function library connecting to ESR and it is imported successfully. Mapping is deployed but the function library deployment is failing with the below error -

[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][GenerationFailed] : The generation and build of the artifact were unsuccessful. Please address the issues outlined below and redeploy the artifact. Generation and build failed for B2B_Migration_Function_Library as validation of resource is failed Validation failed for the method concatTwoQueuesToOne defined in the Java class UDFNodePool.java. When the executionType is ALL_VALUES_OF_CONTEXT, there must be at least 3 arguments of which the last argument must always be of type Container and the second last must always be a ResultList. Validation failed for the method createContextsForFixedBlockSize defined in the Java class UDFNodePool.java. When the executionType is ALL_VALUES_OF_CONTEXT, except the last and second last arguments, all others must be of type String [], float [], or int []. Validation failed for the method buildBlocksAndGetValueByIndex defined in the Java class UDFNodePool.java. When the executionType is ALL_VALUES_OF_CONTEXT, except the last and second last arguments, all others must be of type String [], float [], or int [].

Can someone please suggest how this can be rectified? 

Thanks,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: How to create a archive job in SAP PIPO for a specific interface?
entry.content:
Dear Friends, 

Good day. 

Is there an option available in SAP PIPO (Java Stack) to create archive job for a specific interface? (not all interfaces).  Ours SAP PIPO version is 7.5 SP 31. 

Kindly clarify. 

Thank you. 

Regards, 

Jegatheeswaran P.

entry.title: sap po sftp sender handle positional file with multiple header rows
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is it possible to convert a positional file containing multiple header rows using the standard FCC?

Below is an example of the file structure.

HeaderField1HeaderField2HeaderField3
DetailField1DetailField2DetailField3DetailField4DetailField5
DetailField1DetailField2DetailField3DetailField4DetailField5
DetailField1DetailField2DetailField3DetailField4DetailField5
HeaderField1HeaderField2HeaderField3
DetailField1DetailField2DetailField3DetailField4DetailField5
DetailField1DetailField2DetailField3DetailField4DetailField5


Please note that there may be multiple Detail rows associated with each Header row. In the example below, I expect the FCC to generate an xml with 2 subelements, the first one containing 1 header subelement and 3 detail subelements, the second one containing 1 header subelement and 2 detail subelements.

Thanks,

entry.title: SAP PIPO 7.5 RFC Setup Sender to RFC Receiver
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am currently following this post, but I’m a bit unsure about what values to enter in the following fields and where to find the correct Application Server and Service details.

entry.title: Issue Migrating Integrations to SAP Integration Suite via "Migrate Process Orchestration Artifacts"
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently facing an issue while trying to migrate integrations from SAP Process Orchestration (PO) to SAP Integration Suite using the Migrate Process Orchestration Artifacts tool.

I have successfully configured the connection to the Process Orchestration System, and the connection test is successful. In Step 2 of the migration wizard, I can see and select the integration scenarios available in PO without any issues.

However, in the Pattern step, where I should be able to select an integration pattern, no options are displayed, as shown in the image below:

Has anyone in the community faced a similar issue? Are there any additional prerequisites or configurations required to have the integration patterns show up correctly in this step?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: please propose which tool is perfect one to migrate from PIPO TO SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

Could you please advise on the most suitable tool for migrating from PIPO to SAP CPI? Your recommendation will help us ensure a smooth and efficient transition.
Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: HTTP Communication Error Could not retrieve oAuth Token. REST Sender Adapter Client Credential Grant
entry.content:
Hello, I was facing this problem and because the service I was trying to reach has a proxy API in the middle, was necessary 2 authorizations:
API-Key auth using OAuth 2.0 Additional Parameter (Proxy API)
Client Secret: The end service to be reached

Paramter Type: HttpHeader

Error when make Ping in the Sender REST CC:

But after configuring the CC:

The Module tab not changes:

And finally work! (in my case)

I hope work for you too!

entry.title: SAP PO Java Adapter Error
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I am facing an error with SAP Adapter. 

When I test the SOAP interface on SAP system it runs well. But when I use the exposed SOAP API on SOAPUI or Postman, it cannot send the request, it returns the error ERROR:java.net.SocketException: Connection reset.

Here is the configuration I set on PI:

When I test on SOAPUI, it returns the error:

While it runs well on SAP GUI.

entry.title: The Future of SAP: Latest Trends Shaping the Industry in 2025
entry.content:
The SAP landscape is evolving rapidly. Here are the trends every aspiring consultant should know about:

1. AI & Machine Learning

AI is embedded in SAP solutions today. Consultants who understand AI-driven automation are highly sought after.

2. Cloud-First Deployments

S/4HANA Cloud is becoming standard. Cloud expertise is now a premium skill.

3. Sustainability & ESG Reporting

Organizations need consultants to help achieve ESG compliance. New specialization = new opportunities.

4. Low-Code/No-Code Solutions

Configuration skills are more valuable than ever. Rapid implementation is the new standard.

5. Cybersecurity & Data Privacy

Security expertise commands top dollar. Every project requires privacy compliance.

6. Real-Time Analytics

Business intelligence consultants are in high demand. Data storytelling is critical.

7. Extended Supply Chain Networks

Supply chain visibility is booming. Consultants here are building thriving careers.

8. Agile Methodologies

Waterfall is out. Agile, iterative approaches are the new normal.

The Bottom Line

The most valuable consultants in 2025 will specialize in these emerging areas. Don't just follow trends—master one and differentiate yourself.

Which trend excites you most? Which area do you want to specialize in? Drop your thoughts in the comments below!

Let's grow together in this dynamic industry. What's your next move? #                                                                                      #SAP #Trends2025 #CareerGrowth #SAPConsulting #AI #CloudComputing #DigitalTransformation

entry.title: MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.engine.services.jndi.persistent.exceptions720.Namespac in PI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
We are facing an issue in SAP PI/PO where the message is processed successfully and delivered to the target system, but the sender receives an HTTP 500 error. In the logs we see the following:

MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.engine.services.jndi.persistent.exceptions720.NameNotFoundException: Object not found in lookup of NamespaceMapperBean.

Even though message delivery is successful, this exception still appears and causes the user to see a 500 Internal Server Error.

Could you please advise what could cause this..

Thank you

entry.title: MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.engine.services.jndi.persistent.exceptions720 in PI/PO
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
We are facing an issue in SAP PI/PO where the message is processed successfully and delivered to the target system, but the sender receives an HTTP 500 error. In the logs we see the following:

MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.engine.services.jndi.persistent.exceptions720.NameNotFoundException: Object not found in lookup of NamespaceMapperBean.

Even though message delivery is successful, this exception still appears and causes the user to see a 500 Internal Server Error.

Thanks!

entry.title: CPI HTTP Receiver always returns 400 “Syntax Error” although same JSON works in Postman
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We are trying to call a BZSt REST endpoint from SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) using the HTTP Receiver adapter. The goal is to send a small JSON request from our SAP backend to the German EVATR service and receive a validation response.

The same request (same URL, same JSON body, same token/parameters) works perfectly in Postman. In CPI, however, we consistently receive an HTTP 400 error from the target system.

The error message in the CPI log is:

org.apache.camel.component.ahc.AhcOperationFailedException:
HTTP operation failed invoking https://api.evatr.vies.bzst.de/app/v1/abfrage with statusCode: 400

 The response body from the server is an HTML error page:

<html>
<head>
<base href="/error_path/">
<title>400 Bad Request</title>
</head>
<body>
<h1>Bad Request</h1>
<p>The server detected a syntax error in your request.<br>
Check your request and all parameters.</p>

<p>Your request ID was: aSW1_biQek9iXbaem_MTxwAABZY</p>
</body>
</html>

In the meantime, we have tested quite a lot of variations. We tried many different header combinations, including explicitly setting headers like Content-Type, Accept, Host, and Content-Length, and we experimented with the “Allowed / Request Headers” setting of the HTTP Receiver adapter (including using *). None of these attempts changed the behavior – CPI still only seems to forward a very small set of headers, and additional headers set via a Content Modifier often do not appear in the actual outbound call (according to the HTTP_Receiver_Adapter_Request_Headers attachment).

We also experimented with the payload itself: different JSON formats, slightly modified test payloads, sending the exact same body as Postman, and even building a minimal iFlow where the JSON body is hard-coded in a Content Modifier and sent directly to the HTTP receiver. The result is always the same 400 response from the BZSt service, so it does not appear to be a simple syntax issue in the JSON itself.

What could be the reason for this behavior, and does anyone know how to resolve it or what else we should check?

Here is how the iFlow is built and the error messages:

entry.title: SAP PO: Empty Request Payload (REST to RFC)
entry.content:
Hi,

There is a requirement to send the request based from URL query params and map it to the target field via UDF, this is already working however I don't see any way to send empty payload as it is still looking for the source payload in Dummy Message Type even if it's not mapped to the target. If I add it it's working if not, I'm getting the following error response:

but If I added the source payload of the dummy message type it is working, although it's only dummy - it seems like it still requires it.

Can you anyone share what approach I can do to achieve the empty request payload?

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP PO system Changes Involved - For S4 Hana RISE Conversion from SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement where SAP ECC conversion is done to S4 HANA RISE. There are around 100 interfaces involved(Adapters - RFC/IDoc/Proxy) which will be impacted and we need to consider the changes in SAP PO.

Approach 1 --> Create new Technical System and New Business system(For S4 HANA RISE) in SLD and create all new interfaces from scratch without touching the current set-up. This may take more time and involve a lot of end to end testing.

Approach 2 --> Create new Technical System (For S4 HANA RISE) and Point to the existing Business System(which was pointing previously to SAP ECC). In this case development work will be saved and less time will be involved.

Is there a possibility on the Approach 2, anyone worked before and suggest if this will work or not?  Do we need to consider some point, which I am missing.

Thanks.

Best Regards,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: ODATA_CALLING_ERROR: How to configure Resource Path in OData Sender Adapter
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on an OData to JDBC integration where we are trying to fetch IDoc status information from SAP and update it on the client side.

This is a CDS view with parameters exposed as ODATA which we are trying to consume from ODATA Sender channel.
We have created an HTTP Destination as shown below:

Destination Name: ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS
URL:
https://agvdwdci01.corp.mooginc.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS_CDS?sap-client=250&sap-language=EN

Resource Path : ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS(p_days=7)/Set
OData Sender Adapter and OData structure snapshots are attached for reference.

However, we are encountering the following error in the PO channel:

ODATA_CALLING_ERROR: OData call failed
404 : NOT_FOUND [ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS(p_days=7)/Set]

The same destination works correctly when tested directly in the browser as well as via Postman.

Could someone please advise what the correct Resource Path should be in the OData channel configuration to make this work successfully?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SAP PO - BPM error
entry.content:
Price Tolerance is completed but has not reflected in Finance, even though it was already approved. When we checked in the Fiori system, we observed that the Price Tolerance approval for PO is still pending. It was queried multiple times and all previous queries were approved, but the last approval was not completed successfully.

Upon investigation, we found that the BPM task was suspended and in error. When we checked the reason for the error, we found the following comments:

------------------------
"Process 'ApprovalRefProcess' suspended Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' set to In Error Mapping failed Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' suspended Process 'PriceTolerance (0)' set to In Error Mapping failed Process 'ApprovalCO' suspended Mapping failed java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Value 'KZN Durban Storage' 9 boxes Xtraspace, 99 Chris Hani Road, Briadene, Durban, 4051 Lionel Thangamuthu 060 588 5815 (CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Sep 23 2025 15:12:35)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Oct 28 2025 06:59:55)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Wed Oct 29 2025 11:18:07)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Oct 31 2025 07:49:25)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Nov 04 2025 08:03:29)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Thu Nov 06 2025 13:11:28)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 07 2025 11:53:11)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 14 2025 11:23:08)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Nov 17 2025 08:39:31)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Tue Nov 18 2025 08:46:46)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 21 2025 10:12:19)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Nov 24 2025 10:16:41)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Thu Nov 27 2025 08:43:33)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Fri Nov 28 2025 11:50:02)(CHRISTINE.FISHER Mon Dec 01 2025 07:26:45) of type http://www.sap.com/caf/oup.com/fpa.bl.serreq.modeled#LongText fails validation check: MaxLength=1100 com.sap.mapping.lib.execution.api.exception.TExecutionException: Cannot process assignment RelayTransformer, VoidExpander( TerminalPart.assignment ) at ReqInternalText( TerminalPart.assignment ) at Header( BroadPart.child ) at updateRequest( NarrowPart.child ) at com.sap.caf.oup.com/fpa.bl.serreq.oup/com/caf/app/serr/serreqservice:updateHeaderItemDataV1( BroadPart.child ) at ( NarrowMapping ) For more details, decrease severity of log controller as described in SAP Note 1755360. ---------------------------------------------------------------------

We identified that the failure occurred due to the maximum length validation error. To resolve this, we edited the PO 'header note' in ME22N in SAP and tried to resume the BPM task, but it failed again for the same reason. We then asked the user to resubmit the Price Tolerance for approval, but in the Fiori page, the option to submit for approval was disabled. To address this, we canceled the BPM task for the PO.

Requirement - User need to get this Price Tolerance approved and it should be reflected in Finance.

entry.title: Elimination of SAP PI PO systems
entry.content:
Hello. I'm creating this post so we can all share our opinions constructively.

I recently learned that SAP plans to eliminate SAP PI/PO systems to focus solely on Integration Suite.

As someone who works extensively with PO, I feel saddened and angry with SAP. After all these years of
working and continuously acquiring knowledge in PO, SAP seems to have decided to kick it to the curb.
All that knowledge feels like throwing it away. Similarly, SAP is forcing clients who have already invested
money in implementing PO to migrate to Integration Suite. I believe many clients don't want the cloud and prefer
to keep their servers on-premises for security or other reasons. However, SAP doesn't care and is forcing all
clients to move their infrastructure to the cloud... and of course, spend money again.
Personally, I find this despicable.

And although I work with PO, I also work with Integration Suite. I find IS awful, much more difficult,
since you have to develop the business logic by creating huge and complicated iFlows, while in PO,
integrations are generally point-to-point, making them easier to implement and understand.
And then there are options like the Monitor, which is rubbish compared to the PO monitor, where it shows
you all the messages, their log traces, payloads... without you having to do anything, by default.
In IS, if you want to see anything in the monitor, you have to develop it yourself to save headers,
log files, payloads, etc., wasting your time.

Anyway, I find it incredible that SAP has played this game with its customers, forcing them to switch.
And of course, it's forcing us, the employees, to learn another way of doing integrations, throwing away
our knowledge. (I just hope SAP doesn't do very well.)

What do you think?
What's your opinion?
Are you for or against it?

Let's see if we can start a thread to discuss this and get it to someone at SAP.

Thanks. Regards.

entry.title: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - SAP S4 to Biztalk 2016
entry.content:
Hello Team,

While Connecting from SAP S4 Cloud to MS Biztalk 2016 it is failing with below error. Totally stucked up with this error for few weeks. SAP states to uplift SAP NCO connector to 3.1.6 it doesn't really help.

 

Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - Stucked in SM58

Latest Update from SAP- Really issue is strange and fishy!!!

Logs:

 

MS REPLY:

 

 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS
